riskFactors: No explicit on-chain proof-of-reserves oracle was identified; reserve verification relies on Paxos transparency and attestation reporting rather than live on-chain PoR.
No standalone public legal opinion URL was found; legal evidence relies on Paxos Terms and Conditions plus regulator materials from NYDFS and OCC.
No dedicated smart-contract audit report URL was identified in the current layer set; smartContractRisk remains below institutional-grade despite verified Etherscan code.
Holder concentration data beyond Ethereum holder count is not included; concentrationRisk remains conservative.
Direct purchase, conversion, and redemption through Paxos are account-based and subject to verification, jurisdictional restrictions, and Paxos terms.
Market data is time-sensitive and mostly aggregator-derived; price, market cap, volume, and TVL should be refreshed before production publication.
Allocated physical gold redemption has a high threshold of 430 PAXG plus applicable fees, which limits direct physical redemption accessibility for smaller holders.
mitigants: PAXG is issued and managed by Paxos Trust Company, N.A., with strong regulatory context from OCC and NYDFS materials.
PAXG is backed by allocated physical London Good Delivery gold, with each PAXG representing one fine troy ounce of gold according to official Paxos disclosures.
Paxos provides an official PAXG transparency page and attestation reports, with KPMG LLP identified as attestation provider in the current source trail.
PAXG has a verified Ethereum ERC-20 contract and a public Etherscan token tracker for supply, holder count, and activity review.
PAXG has strong secondary-market liquidity across major centralized exchanges and tracked market venues.
Paxos discloses AML/KYC, OFAC/government watchlist screening, customer due diligence, and transaction monitoring controls.
PAXG Terms define account-based redemption/conversion pathways into USD, unallocated gold, and allocated physical gold.
assessmentMethod: Evidence-based Nexus RWA manual risk assessment using completed identity, blockchain, reserve, institutional, compliance, market, yield, liquidity, and sources layers. Scores are conservative where public smart-contract audit, on-chain proof-of-reserves oracle, legal opinion, or holder concentration evidence is incomplete.
